matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: PPARG dominant-negative model (PPARG severe insulin-resistance model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a PPARG adipogenesis and insulin-sensitivity thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to lipid storage, adipocyte differentiation, metabolic inflammation, type 2 diabetes traits, or PPAR-gamma agonist pharmacology. Common markers remain probabilistic modifiers.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed rs1801282 (PPARG Pro12Ala) dosage suggests this sample carries the PPARG prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a PPARG adipogenesis and insulin-sensitivity thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to lipid storage, adipocyte differentiation, metabolic inflammation, type 2 diabetes traits, or PPAR-gamma agonist pharmacology. Common markers remain probabilistic modifiers. This specific marker is curated as PPARG adipogenesis/insulin-sensitivity marker. rs1801282 (PPARG Pro12Ala) is curated as a PPARG marker for Adipogenesis and insulin-sensitivity biology, Type 2 diabetes and metabolic trait association research. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the nuclear receptor transcriptional control of adipogenesis, insulin sensitivity, lipid storage, inflammation, and thiazolidinedione pharmacology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs1801282 (PPARG Pro12Ala) at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the PPARG prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag nuclear receptor transcriptional control of adipogenesis, insulin sensitivity, lipid storage, inflammation, and thiazolidinedione pharmacology.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a PPARG adipogenesis and insulin-sensitivity thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to lipid storage, adipocyte differentiation, metabolic inflammation, type 2 diabetes traits, or PPAR-gamma agonist pharmacology. Common markers remain probabilistic modifiers. An observed PPARG variant places this sample in the curated PPARG research context. The local PPARG bundle is metabolic and pharmacology oriented. Common PPARG markers are low-penetrance modifiers, while rare dominant-negative variants require clinical classification. Pro12Ala is a widely studied PPARG functional/metabolic marker with context-dependent effects on insulin sensitivity and metabolic traits.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: Type 2 diabetes and metabolic trait association research

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a PPARG variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a PPARG adipogenesis and insulin-sensitivity thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to lipid storage, adipocyte differentiation, metabolic inflammation, type 2 diabetes traits, or PPAR-gamma agonist pharmacology. Common markers remain probabilistic modifiers. PPARG methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a nuclear receptor transcriptional control of adipogenesis, insulin sensitivity, lipid storage, inflammation, and thiazolidinedione pharmacology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local PPARG bundle is metabolic and pharmacology oriented. Common PPARG markers are low-penetrance modifiers, while rare dominant-negative variants require clinical classification. Pro12Ala is a widely studied PPARG functional/metabolic marker with context-dependent effects on insulin sensitivity and metabolic traits.
